response.justification: The candidate is a broadly experienced, craftsmanship-oriented senior engineer with strong full-stack and some systems-level background, but he falls well short of the core minimum qualifications for this role. His C++ experience is limited to a short stint at National Instruments and hobby/thesis projects rather than the required 8 years, his infrastructure experience is meaningful but narrow (roughly two years of low-latency DLang microservices at Sociomantic), and he has no formal technical-leadership role owning a cloud compute platform or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers at scale.
strengths: Real high-throughput systems experience: low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering-quality culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus a track record of teaching, workshops, and mentoring
Breadth and ownership: 15+ years across web, mobile, embedded, and infra, including founding and operating an open-source Kubernetes-deployed platform
concerns: C++ depth far below the 8-year minimum; primary languages are TypeScript/Node.js and DLang
No dedicated cloud compute platform or large-scale distributed-infrastructure leadership role; cloud exposure is application-level (AWS CDK, Kubernetes)
Technical leadership is informal (workshops, side-project founder) rather than 2+ years formally leading and mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers
